title: ANS00434 Pulmonary Embolism Caused by DVT
text_en: In some DVT cases, a blood clot may detach from a deep vein and join the bloodstream. The clot travels from the deep veins of the leg through the veins of the abdomen, into the heart, and finally lodges in the pulmonary artery or its branches, blocking blood flow to the lungs.

title: ANS00434 Pulmonary Embolism Caused by DVT
text_en: This condition, called pulmonary embolism, is very serious and may permanently damage a portion of the lung. If a clot is large or if there are many clots, pulmonary embolism may also cause death.

title: ANS00434 Pulmonary Embolism Caused by DVT
text_en: If you experience symptoms of PE, please tell your doctor or nurse immediately. These symptoms may include, shortness of breath, a sudden sharp pain in the chest, a feeling of lightheadedness or dizziness, rapid or irregular heartbeat, or coughing that produces
